steps.expected: recommendation_reason mentions gas-adjusted comparison or fallback note about missing gas
steps.observed: recommendation_reason: 'Spandex (kyberswap) outputs 0.000153 WETH more (+0.030%). Gas estimates unavailable for Curve, comparing raw output only.' — Correctly falls back to raw output comparison with missing-gas note when Curve has no gas_used.

steps.action: Inspect source code for gas-adjusted comparison logic (src/server.ts lines 248-330)
steps.expected: Three code paths: (1) both have gas + ETH output → subtract gas from output, (2) both have gas + non-ETH output → note gas costs in reason, (3) missing gas → fallback to raw output with note
steps.observed: All three paths verified in source. ETH output path computes spandexAdjustedOutput = spandexOutput - spandexGasCostEth and compares. Non-ETH path notes gas costs. Missing gas path adds 'Gas estimates unavailable for X, comparing raw output only.'

steps.action: Verify unit tests cover gas reversal scenario
steps.expected: Test exists where higher raw output router loses after gas adjustment
steps.observed: Test 'GET /compare recommendation flips when gas costs reverse the output advantage' in server.quote-compare.test.ts verifies Spandex with 1.50 ETH output + 15M gas loses to Curve with 1.49 ETH + 10K gas after adjustment.

steps.action: Navigate to UI, submit USDC→WETH comparison on Ethereum
steps.expected: Recommendation reason visible in UI with gas context
steps.observed: UI displays: 'Spandex (odos) outputs 0.000011 WETH more (+0.002%). Gas estimates unavailable for Curve, comparing raw output only.' with 'Gas: 0.0363 gwei' below.

evidence.network: GET /compare -> 200, recommendation_reason contains 'Gas estimates unavailable for Curve, comparing raw output only', gas_price_gwei: 0.0340, spandex.gas_used: 714466, curve.gas_used: null

assertions.title: Symmetric gas display for both routers

steps.action: Navigate to UI, submit USDC→WETH comparison on Ethereum, expand Spandex details
steps.expected: Gas Used field visible with numeric value
steps.observed: Gas Used: 716697 displayed in Spandex quote details

steps.action: Switch to Curve tab, expand details
steps.expected: Gas Used field visible showing 'N/A' when gas unavailable
steps.observed: Gas Used: N/A displayed in Curve quote details (no sender provided, so gas estimation unavailable)

steps.action: Verify source code renders Gas Used symmetrically (src/server.ts line 1783-1784)
steps.expected: Both router quote displays use same Gas Used field with value or 'N/A' fallback
steps.observed: Code: const gasUsed = data.gas_used && Number(data.gas_used) > 0 ? data.gas_used : null; details.push('Gas Used' + (gasUsed || 'N/A')). Applied identically to both Spandex and Curve quote rendering.

evidence.network: GET /compare -> 200, spandex.gas_used: 714466, curve.gas_used: null (displayed as N/A in UI)

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed. VAL-TRADE-001 confirmed gas-adjusted comparison logic works correctly — falls back to raw output with missing-gas note when Curve lacks gas data, and source code + unit tests verify the full gas-adjusted path for ETH output. VAL-TRADE-004 confirmed symmetric Gas Used display: Spandex shows numeric value (716697), Curve shows 'N/A', using identical rendering code.
